results.possibleIdentity: Julius Soled was a mechanical engineer and professional engineer (PE) who was active in the late 1950s through the late 1980s. He was employed by the United States Army at the Signal Corps Engineering Laboratories and the Electronics Command (ECOM) at Fort Monmouth, New Jersey. Professionally, he was a specialist in industrial fasteners and technical devices, authoring the widely recognized "Fasteners Handbook" (1957) and the "Pictorial Handbook of Technical Devices" (1959). During the late 1960s, he served as an engineer for Project Mallard, a significant international tactical communications project. Based in Asbury Park, New Jersey, Soled was a member of the Asbury Park Toastmasters and the IEEE. He demonstrated a strong interest in the intersection of art and engineering; he was described in a 1994 Stevens Institute of Technology publication as an artist who assembled a massive collection of art works into multi-volume binders. In his later years, he also contributed to research in technical-spiritual fields such as chromotherapy and psychotronics, presenting on these topics in the mid-1980s.
